design_philosophy.core_loop: Point-and-click puzzle solving with time travel mechanics; select verb command, apply to world object, observe consequences across three time periods (past, present, future)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Preventing Purple Tentacle's global domination by manipulating cause-and-effect across time periods; solving interconnected puzzles that require understanding temporal consequences
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Detective-detective solving elaborate logic puzzles through exploration and experimentation; wielding time travel as a puzzle tool; discovering how small actions ripple through history
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits fully to comedic tone and cartoon sensibility (inspired by Chuck Jones); refuses puzzle-game punishment—no death states, no time pressure, emphasis on exploration and humor over difficulty; uses time-travel not as narrative gimmick but as core puzzle architecture; multiple character perspectives solve same puzzles differently, encouraging revisiting locations; integrates full playable Maniac Mansion game-within-game as easter egg

difficulty.checkpointing: Manual save system; player controls when to save progress; no failure states prevent progression
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No difficulty settings documented; puzzle solutions are deterministic but allow multiple approaches; no time limits or resource scarcity creates tension; player can experiment without penalty, supporting learning through failure

progression.unlock_mechanism: Puzzle completion unlocks time-period access; solving puzzles in one time period affects availability of items/puzzle solutions in other periods; characters separated by time machine malfunction create three parallel progression tracks (past/present/future)
replayability_hooks: Multiple character perspectives on same puzzles (Bernard, Hoagie, Laverne have different inventory spaces and unique interaction options)
Time-travel causality invites experimentation: discovering how past actions alter future state
Embedded full Maniac Mansion game playable on in-game computer (unprecedented for its era)
Item aging and temporal object-sharing mechanics (Chron-o-Johns as cross-time inventory transport)
Speedrun community active (38 documented runs, sub-16 minute completions via route optimization)

world_building_techniques: Cartoon-style visuals establishing comedic tone (artist Peter Chan)
Interaction with historical American figures (John Hancock, Thomas Jefferson, George Washington) integrated into puzzle logic, not cutscenes
Environmental storytelling via time-period contrasts: same locations shown under Purple Tentacle's tyranny (future) vs. normal operation (past/present)
Character personality expressed through unique dialogue and interaction options rather than exposition
Plot framing: five-year sequel gap from Maniac Mansion provides context but not required for understanding

faction_indicators: Purple Tentacle's authoritarian future regime (enslaved humanity, mansion-based dictatorship)
Dr. Fred's mad scientist ideology (control through technology/containment)
Green Tentacle represents alternative: peaceful, non-aggressive sapience
danger_signals: Purple Tentacle's intelligence mutation caused by industrial toxin runoff (technology/pollution danger)
Sludge-o-Matic machine represents unchecked industrial risk
Time-travel intervention as necessity: cannot negotiate with or reform Purple Tentacle, only prevent his origin
environmental_storytelling: Edison family motel: site of both creation and potential redemption across time periods
Toxic river behind lab: environmental consequence become existential threat
Future timeline shows oppressed world under single-entity control, establishing stakes for puzzle-solving
Historical locations (colonial America) repurposed for mundane puzzle contexts, creating cognitive dissonance that reinforces comedy
